title: Best Wells Fargo credit cards for July 2026
full_text:
Wells Fargo offers various credit card options for every type of consumer, whether you want to earn cash or travel rewards, or are looking for a long introductory APR offer. Even better, many Wells Fargo cards provide cell phone protection as a standard benefit.

Best Wells Fargo credit cards for July 2026

Wells Fargo Active Cash® Credit Card: Best overall

Learn more Wells Fargo Active Cash® Card Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $0

Welcome offer Earn a $200 cash rewards bonus after spending $500 within the first 3 months

Introductory APR 0% intro APR for 12 months from account opening on qualifying balance transfers (balance transfers made within 120 days qualify for the intro APR and intro balance transfer fee; 18.49%, 24.49%, or 28.49% variable APR after that)

Purchase APR 18.49%, 24.49%, or 28.49% variable APR

Introductory Balance Transfer APR 0% intro APR for 12 months from account opening on qualifying balance transfers

Ongoing Balance Transfer APR 18.49%, 24.49%, or 28.49% Variable APR Rewards rate Unlimited 2% cash rewards on purchases Benefits Get up to $600 of cell phone protection against damage or theft ($25 deductible applies) Pay-per-use 24/7 roadside dispatch assistance Auto rental collision damage waiver coverage



Why we like it: The Wells Fargo Active Cash hits the right notes on multiple fronts, including no annual fee, a straightforward rewards rate, and multiple intro APR offers. This can easily be the one card you carry in your wallet if you like to travel light.

Wells Fargo Reflect® Card: Best for 0% intro APR

Learn more Wells Fargo Reflect® Card Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $0

Welcome offer None

Purchase APR 17.49%, 23.99%, or 28.24% variable APR

Introductory APR 0% intro APR for 21 months from account opening on purchases and qualifying balance transfers (balance transfers made within 120 days qualify for the intro APR and intro balance transfer fee; 17.49%, 23.99%, or 28.24% variable APR after that)

Introductory Balance Transfer APR 0% intro APR for 21 months from account opening on qualifying balance transfers

Ongoing Balance Transfer APR 17.49%, 23.99%, or 28.24% Variable APR Rewards rate None

Benefits Get up to $600 of cell phone protection against damage or theft ($25 deductible applies) 0% introductory APR is useful to finance large purchases or transfer a balance from another card



Why we like it: The Wells Fargo Reflect provides multiple long introductory APR offers, giving you the opportunity to finance an upcoming large purchase or help pay down debt without worrying about interest during the offer period. It doesn't provide many other benefits, but that's okay because its main focus is on the 0% intro APR offers.

Read our full review of the Wells Fargo Reflect Card

Wells Fargo Autograph® Card: Best for everyday earning

Learn more Wells Fargo Autograph® Card Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $0

Welcome offer Earn 20,000 bonus points after spending $1,000 within the first 3 months (worth a $200 cash redemption value)

Introductory APR 0% intro APR on purchases for the first 12 months, 18.49%, 24.49%, or 28.49% variable APR after

Purchase APR 18.49%, 24.49%, or 28.49% variable APR Rewards rate 3x points on dining, travel, gas and EV charging stations, transit, popular streaming services, and phone plans 1x points on all other purchases

Benefits Get up to $600 of cell phone protection against damage or theft (up to two claims per year, $25 deductible applies) Auto rental collision damage waiver coverage Visa Signature® perks, including Visa Signature® Concierge service and Luxury Hotel Collection privileges



Why we like it: The Wells Fargo Autograph makes sense if you want an affordable card for earning travel rewards on everyday purchases. With its high rewards rate in multiple common categories, you can rack up points on purchases you already make in your day-to-day life.

Read our full review of the Wells Fargo Autograph Card

Wells Fargo Autograph Journey℠ Card: Best for travel

Learn more Wells Fargo Autograph Journey℠ Card Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $95

Welcome offer Earn 60,000 bonus points when you spend $4,000 in purchases in the first 3 months

Purchase APR 19.49%, 25.49%, or 28.49% variable APR

Ongoing Balance Transfer APR 19.49%, 25.49%, or 28.49% Variable APR Rewards rate 5x points with hotels 4x points with airlines 3x points on restaurants and other travel 1x points on everything else

Benefits Get a $50 annual statement credit with $50 minimum airline purchase Access travel protections such as lost baggage reimbursement, travel accident insurance, and trip cancellation protection Get up to $1,000 of cell phone protection against damage, theft, or loss (up to two claims every 12 months; $25 deductible applies)



Why we like it: The Wells Fargo Autograph Journey is an excellent card for travelers who want to keep their membership costs low while still benefiting from useful travel perks, such as no foreign transaction fees and travel insurance. The Autograph Journey also provides elevated earning rates on travel and dining purchases, giving frequent travelers more bang for their buck on applicable expenses.

Read our full review of the Wells Fargo Autograph Journey Card

Choice Privileges® Select Mastercard®: Best for hotels

Learn more Choice Privileges® Select Mastercard® Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $95

Welcome offer Earn 60,000 bonus points when you spend $3,000 in purchases in the first 3 months — enough to redeem for up to 7 reward nights at select Choice Hotels® properties (additional fees and terms may apply)

Purchase APR See issuer site for current APR Rewards rate 10x points on stays at participating Choice Hotels® properties, plus on Choice Privileges point purchases 5x points on purchases at gas stations, grocery stores, home improvement stores, and on phone plan services 1x points on other purchases

Benefits Earn 30,000 bonus points every account anniversary, enough to redeem up to 3 reward nights at Choice Hotels properties (additional fees and terms may apply) Upgrade to higher Choice Privileges Elite status and earn rewards faster with points earned using your card on everyday purchases Earn rewards more frequently throughout the year — such as bonus points or gift cards



Why we like it: Simply put, if you stay at Choice Privileges hotels, you should consider the Choice Privileges Select Mastercard. This card provides specific hotel benefits for cardholders, including automatic Platinum Elite membership, a 30,000-point anniversary bonus, and Milestone Rewards.

Wells Fargo Signify Business Cash® Card: Best for business owners

Learn more Signify Business Cash® Card Rates & fees, terms apply Annual fee $0

Welcome offer Earn a $500 cash rewards bonus after spending $5,000 within the first 3 months

Introductory APR 0% intro APR on purchases for the first 12 months, after which the standard APR applies

Purchase APR See issuer site for current APR Rewards rate 2% cash rewards on all eligible purchases Benefits 0% introductory APR is useful to finance large purchases Redeem your rewards as a statement credit, for gift cards, travel, and more Complimentary membership to the Priority Pass airport lounge network (lounge visit fees apply)



Why we like it: The Wells Fargo Signify Business Cash is a straightforward cash-back card specifically designed for business owners who value simplicity. With no annual fee, a flat rewards rate, and the option to use a 0% intro APR offer, you can focus on your business rather than a complicated credit card product.

More Wells Fargo credit cards to consider

Wells Fargo Attune® Card

One Key™ Card

One Key+™ Card

Choice Privileges® Mastercard®

What to look for in a Wells Fargo credit card

Welcome offer: Not all Wells Fargo cards provide welcome offers, but many do, and they can be excellent ways for qualified applicants to earn a lot of rewards soon after account opening.

Annual fee: Wells Fargo credit cards typically have low or no annual fees, making them much more affordable than many other available card options.

Perks and benefits: Many Wells Fargo cards provide cell phone protection, but you would have to compare other specific benefits between cards to see what aligns best with your lifestyle. For example, the Wells Fargo Autograph Journey provides multiple travel insurance perks, including trip cancellation and interruption protection.

Rewards: The best rewards rate depends on your spending habits and preferences. In general, the Wells Fargo Autograph has some of the highest earning potential on everyday purchases, but the Wells Fargo Active Cash makes it easy to earn cash rewards across a wide range of categories.

Intro APR offers: Certain Wells Fargo credit cards provide 0% introductory APR offers, allowing eligible cardholders opportunities to finance purchases or help pay down debt, depending on the available offer.

Types of Wells Fargo credit cards

Wells Fargo offers various types of credit cards to suit different goals and needs.

Cash-back

These cards earn cash rewards on eligible purchases. You can typically redeem cash rewards for account credits, covering eligible past purchases, gift cards, and checking out online with PayPal (where applicable).

Wells Fargo offers these cash-back credit cards:

Wells Fargo Active Cash Credit Card

Wells Fargo Attune® Card

Wells Fargo Signify Business Cash Card

Read more: The best cash-back credit cards

0% intro APR

These cards provide 0% introductory APR offers on qualifying balance transfers, purchases, or both. That means you don't have to pay interest on purchases or a qualifying balance transfer during the offer period, though standard APR rates apply after the period ends. Qualifying balance transfers also come with balance transfer fees.

Wells Fargo offers these 0% intro APR credit cards:

Read more: The best 0% APR credit cards

Travel

Frequent travelers will find these cards appealing, as they provide travel rewards on eligible purchases. Some of them also have specific travel benefits, such as travel insurance coverage, and they don't charge foreign transaction fees.

Wells Fargo offers these travel credit cards:

Read more: The best travel credit cards

Business

Business credit cards make sense for small business owners who want to take advantage of business-specific benefits, such as earning rewards on business expenses. In addition, it's an easy way to separate business expenses from personal spending to help with doing your taxes.

Wells Fargo offers this business credit card:

Wells Fargo Signify Business Cash Card

Read more: The best business credit cards

Should I open a Wells Fargo credit card?

It may make sense to apply for a Wells Fargo credit card if:

You're already a Wells Fargo customer

You prefer credit cards with low or no annual fees

You want to take advantage of a 0% intro APR offer

You like straightforward rewards rates

You want a card with cell phone protection

You may want to consider other cards if:

You want premium travel benefits, such as airport lounge access

You want a specific airline credit card

Wells Fargo transfer partners don't align with your travel preferences

Wells Fargo credit card FAQs

What is the best Wells Fargo card to get?

These are some of the best Wells Fargo credit cards for different situations:

What credit score do you need for a Wells Fargo credit card?

You typically need at least a good credit score to qualify for a Wells Fargo credit card. That's at least a 670 score, according to the FICO scoring model. Keep in mind that card issuers may also consider other factors, such as your income and history of repaying debt, to determine your creditworthiness when applying for a credit card.

Does Wells Fargo ever raise your credit limit?

Wells Fargo may automatically increase your credit limit through periodic reviews of your account activity and other factors. You may also be able to request a credit limit increase, though you should be aware that this could result in a hard inquiry.

Our methodology

We use separate rubrics for different card categories, including Cash Back, Travel, and 0% APR, to give credit cards from major card issuers their own ratings. The specific factors vary by rubric, but may include details about annual fees, rewards rates, 0% introductory APR offers, and more.

We selected the top-rated Wells Fargo credit cards from our existing rubrics to create our list of the best Wells Fargo credit cards. We included various cards to cover a broad range of categories we believe will suit the different needs of potential cardholders.

Editorial Disclosure: The information in this article has not been reviewed or approved by any advertiser. All opinions belong solely to Yahoo Finance and are not those of any other entity. The details on financial products, including card rates and fees, are accurate as of the publish date. All products or services are presented without warranty. Check the bank's website for the most current information. This site doesn't include all currently available offers. Credit score alone does not guarantee or imply approval for any financial product.
